title: Display of Road Licence and Evidence of Motor Vehicle Insurance Regulations, 1979
title_en: Display of Road Licence and Evidence of Motor Vehicle Insurance Regulations, 1979

provisions.title: Citation
provisions.content: These Regulations may be cited as the Display of Road Licence and Evidence of Motor Vehicle Insurance Regulations.

provisions.title: Insurer to issue a certificate in the prescribed form
provisions.content: The National Insurance Corporation of Tanzania Limited shall, in respect of every motor vehicle insured by it, issue a certificate in the form prescribed in the Schedule to these Regulations.

provisions.title: Certificate to be evidence of road licence and motor vehicle insurance
provisions.content: A certificate issued under regulation 2 shall be evidence of the fact that— (a) a road licence has been taken out in respect of the motor vehicle named in the certificate, in compliance with the provisions of the Road Traffic Act 1 ; and 1 Cap. 168 (b) the motor vehicle specified in the certificate has been insured in accordance with the provisions of the Motor Vehicles Insurance Act 2 . 2 Cap. 169

provisions.title: Duty to display certificate
provisions.content: (1) Every owner or person in charge of a motor vehicle which is required to be insured in accordance with the provisions of the Motor Vehicles Insurance Act 3 and licensed in accordance with the provisions of the Road Traffic Act 4 , shall, at all times when that motor vehicle is being used on the road, display on the motor vehicle, in the manner specified in regulation 5 , a certificate issued under regulation 2 . 3 Cap. 169 4 Cap. 168 (2) The owner or the person in charge of a motor vehicle shall on demand, permit any police officer, the Registrar of Motor Vehicles or any person authorised by the Registrar of Motor Vehicles in that behalf to examine the certificate.

provisions.title: Display of certificate
provisions.content: A certificate issued under these regulations shall be displayed on a motor vehicle in the manner specified below— (a) in the case of a motor cycle, in a conspicuous position on the near side of the vehicle in front of the driving seat; (b) in the case of a motor cycle with a side car, on the near side of the handle bar of the cycle or the near side of the combination in front of the driving seat; and (c) in the case of all other motor vehicles, on the near side of the vehicle facing towards the near side of the road and not less than seventy-five centimetres nor more than one and half metre from the ground level between two parallel lines, the first drawn vertically through the rearmost part of the driving seat, and the second drawn vertically centimetres in front of the base of the front glass wind screen is fitted, through a point one metre forward of the first line: Provided that in the case of a motor vehicle fitted with a front glass wind screen extending across the vehicle to the near side, the certificate may be carried facing forwards on the near lower corner of the glass of such wind screen, or within six centimetres of the glass either in front or behind it and so as to be clearly visible from in front at all times by daylight whether such vehicle is moving or stationary.

provisions.title: Offences
provisions.content: Every owner or person in charge of a motor vehicle who— (a) fails to comply with any of the provisions of regulation 4 ; or (b) fails to display a certificate in the manner prescribed in regulation 5 ; or (c) displays a certificate which has been rendered illegible, shall be guilty of an offence and shall be liable on conviction to a fine not exceeding fifteen thousand shillings or to imprisonment for a term not exceeding three years or to both the fine and imprisonment.

provisions.title: Revocation of G.N. No. 119 of 1974
provisions.content: [Revokes the Road Traffic (Display of Certificate of Insurance) Regulations.] Schedule Form [Editorial note: The form has not been reproduced] History of this document 31 July 2002 this version Consolidation Related documents More documents like this one [] [] [] [] ▲ To the top > Contact us Visit our Twitter/X profile Visit our Linked In profile Visit our YouTube channel About TanzLII TanzLII is operated by the Judiciary of Tanzania and the Office of the Attorney General, Chief Parliamentary Draftsman, and publishes the law of Tanzania for free online access for all.
